meta.title: Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces | Camino de Cruces National Park
meta.description: Explore Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces in Panama Province, Panama. A national park with rich biodiversity and historical heritage, ideal for hiking and ecotourism.

hero.title: Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces
hero.subtitle: Camino de Cruces National Park · Panama

intro.title: Explore Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces
intro.description: Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces is located in Panama Province, Panama. It is an important national park with rich biodiversity and historical heritage, ideal for hiking and ecotourism. The park preserves the historic Camino de Cruces trail, an important route connecting Panama City and Colón during the colonial period.

visitGuide.title: Visiting Tips
items: Park is open daily, recommended to visit during daytime
Bring a camera to capture the rich tropical rainforest landscapes
You can combine your visit with nearby attractions like Panama Canal

alsoKnownAs.title: Highlights
items: Rich Biodiversity: The park has rich tropical rainforest ecosystems, ideal for bird watching and ecological observation.
Historical Heritage: Preserves the colonial Camino de Cruces trail, which has important historical value.
Beautiful Natural Scenery: The park is shaded by green trees and has a beautiful environment, making it an ideal place to relax.
Close to Tourist Attractions: Not far from famous attractions like Panama Canal and Panama City.

route.title: Tour Route
route.overview: The ideal route to explore Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces:
steps: Enter from the main entrance, get a map and understand the park's historical background.
Hike along the historic trail and feel the transportation route during the colonial period.
Observe the rich tropical rainforest ecosystem, watch for various birds and plants.
Rest at viewpoints inside the park and enjoy the beautiful scenery of nature.
supplements: It's recommended to go during the day when the light is best, suitable for taking photos and observing wildlife.
You can combine your visit with nearby historical attractions like Panama Canal.
Please respect the natural environment, keep the park clean, and do not disturb wildlife.

knowledge.title: Discover the Charm of Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces

sections.title: Historical Significance
sections.content: The Camino de Cruces trail was an important route connecting Panama City and Colón during the colonial period, with important historical value. This national park protects this important historical heritage.

sections.title: Natural Landscape
sections.content: The park has rich tropical rainforest ecosystems, an excellent place for observing wildlife and plants, showing Panama's rich biodiversity.

sections.title: Visiting Experience
sections.content: Hiking in Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces, feeling Panama's natural beauty and historical heritage, is an unforgettable ecological and cultural experience.

hours.title: Opening Hours
hours.summerTime: Dec-Apr: Pleasant weather, ideal season for hiking
hours.winterTime: May-Nov: Afternoon showers are common, recommended to visit in the morning
hours.warningTime: Recommended to verify latest information before visiting
hours.tip: Daytime visits offer the best experience

tickets.title: Useful Information
tickets.studentsPrice: Self-driving or joining a tour group recommended
tickets.cardPrice: Comfortable hiking shoes, mosquito repellent, and sufficient water recommended. You can combine with nearby Panama Canal.
tickets.officialNotice: For the latest visiting information, please refer to the
tickets.officialNoticeLinkLabel: Ministry of Environment of Panama Official Website

transport.title: Transportation
transport.airportDesc: About 30 minutes drive from Tocumen International Airport (PTY). Renting a car or taking a taxi is recommended.
transport.selfDriveDesc: Located in Panama Province. Simply input 'Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces' in navigation. Parking available at park entrance.
transport.busDesc: Take Panama City buses to nearby area, then take a taxi or walk to park entrance.
transport.intercityDesc: You can join a tour group from Panama City to the national park, hassle-free.
transport.cyclingDesc: Self-driving or joining a tour group recommended. The park is suitable for hiking and exploration.
transport.tipsDesc: Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces is an important place to experience Panama's nature and history. Visiting during daytime is recommended, wear comfortable hiking shoes.

gallery.subtitle: The Charm of Parque Nacional Camino de Cruces

items.name: Laura G.

officialManagement.title: About Parque Natural Cerro Verde
officialManagement.text: Parque Natural Cerro Verde is a famous natural attraction in the Santa Ana Department, boasting spectacular mountain views and rich natural and cultural value. It is directly managed and maintained by the Salvadoran Institute of Tourism (ISTU).

historyTimeline.title: Historical Timeline

items.title: Colonial Period: Important Transportation Route
items.description: The Camino de Cruces trail was an important route connecting Panama City and Colón during the colonial period, used for transporting valuable items like Quetzal feathers.

items.title: 20th Century: Environmental Protection
items.description: With increasing environmental awareness, this area was gradually established as a national park to protect its rich biodiversity and historical heritage.

items.title: 21st Century: Ecotourism Destination
items.description: In the 21st century, with its rich natural landscapes and historical value, it has attracted a large number of ecotourists and become an important destination for Panama's ecotourism.

historyTimeline.guideContent: When visiting, you can start by entering from the main entrance, get a map and understand the park's historical background; then hike along the historic trail and feel the transportation route during the colonial period; if you have enough time, observe the rich tropical rainforest ecosystem and wildlife. Finally, rest at viewpoints inside the park and feel Panama's natural beauty and historical heritage.
